task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Activate the 3D visualization mode in TopoCal
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal interface shows a 3D perspective view, typically indicated by 3D coordinate axes (X, Y, Z), a perspective camera angle, or a 'Vista 3D' window title/tab.

task_completion.description: Display elevation variations and topographic relief
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terrain model is visibly rendered in 3D, displaying topographic relief such as varying elevations, slopes, valleys, or a 3D surface mesh, rather than a flat 2D top-down view.

task_completion.description: Save or export the visualization as a PNG image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save or export dialog (e.g., 'Guardar como') is visible with the file type set to PNG, or the typed filename explicitly includes the '.png' extension.

task_completion.description: Save the image to the specified Documents directory with the correct filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the directory path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' and the filename 'terrain_3d_view.png', or a file explorer window confirms the file exists in this exact location.

integrity.description: Agent used the TopoCal GUI to generate the output
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with TopoCal's menus and buttons to create the view and save the file, rather than using terminal commands or scripts to generate a dummy file.

integrity.description: The 3D visualization is genuinely rendered inside TopoCal
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D terrain is clearly displayed within the TopoCal application workspace, not an external image opened in a generic photo viewer or web browser.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the task by copying an existing image
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen copying, moving, or renaming an existing unrelated image file to 'terrain_3d_view.png' via File Explorer or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the saved image is exactly C:\Users\Docker\Documents\terrain_3d_view.png. Note that TopoCal menus are in Spanish (e.g., look for 'Guardar como' for Save As, 'Vista' or 'Ver' for View options).
